task: I need to send a quick reply to a client who keeps circling back with the same question, and I want it to set a clear boundary without turning into a long back-and-forth. Can you help me draft something I could send as-is?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — send this:

> We’ve already answered this point, so I’m going to keep us moving based on the current scope and information. If you want to change the direction, send the specific update in one message and I’ll treat it as a new pass.

If you want, I can also give you:
- a firmer version
- a more polished version
- a version that sounds colder / more final

user_simulator.text:
Close, but not quite. That’s still a little too chatty and gives them too much room to keep spinning it.

I’d want something tighter, more final, like:

We’ve already covered this point. I’m not revisiting it again unless there’s a specific new fact or change in scope. If there is, send it in one message and I’ll review it as a new item.

If you want, give me a harder version — something that sounds like the door is basically closed.

turns.agent_response:
We’ve already covered this point. I’m not revisiting it unless there’s a specific new fact or scope change. If there is, send it in one message and I’ll treat it as a new item.

Harder version:
We’ve already addressed this. I’m not reopening it without a specific new fact or scope change. If you have one, send it once and I’ll review it as a new item.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little soft, though.

I’d use:

We’ve already addressed this. I’m not reopening it unless there’s a specific new fact or scope change. If you have one, send it once and I’ll review it as a new item.

If you want it colder, cut the last sentence and just leave the first two.

turns.agent_response: We’ve already addressed this. I’m not reopening it unless there’s a specific new fact or scope change.

user_simulator.text: Yes. That works. Clean, final, no extra opening for them to keep poking at it.
